metadata.title: Homeostasis During Fasting. II. Hormone Substrate Differences Between Men and Women
metadata.journal: Journal of Clinical Endocrinology and Metabolism
metadata.demographics: Ten normal men and 9 normal women; Four men matched with 4 women in weight and age; Five women fasted in both preovulatory and postovulatory phases

evidence.quote: Ten normal men and 9 normal women were fasted for 72 hr.
Four men, matching 4 of the women in weight and age, were also fasted a second time while receiving ethinylestradiol (0.1 mg twice daily).
Five of the women were fasted in both the preovulatory and postovulatory phases of the menstrual cycle.
The mean lowest glucose concentration in plasma in the male group during the 3 days of starvation was 66.4 ± 2.9 mg/100 ml. The corresponding figure in females was 47.8 ±2.9 mg/100 ml (p < 0.01).
Mean peak glucagon concentrations in women and men were, respectively, 230.0 ± 36.1 mig/ml and 121.8 ± 17.9 nng/ml (p < 0.01).

methods_participants.label: Menstrual Cycle Study - Women
methods_participants.value: 5 women fasted in preovulatory and postovulatory phases

evidence.quote: Five of the women were fasted in both the preovulatory and postovulatory phases of the menstrual cycle.

methods_participants.label: Glucose Assay Precision - Within Assay
methods_participants.value: 15 to 17 days after the last day of menstrual bleeding

evidence.quote: The former study was conducted 4 to 5 days after the last day of menstrual bleeding
No subject had CHO intolerance and hepatic screening tests were normal.

methods_participants.label: Prefasting Diet - Carbohydrate
methods_participants.value: 1 g/kg of body weight protein daily for at least 2 weeks

evidence.quote: Prior to admission, all subjects ingested a diet containing a minimum of 150 g of carbyhodrate and 1 g/kg of body weight of protein for at least 2 weeks.
Each 3-day fast was preceded by a 2-day control period.
Samples were obtained at 8 AM and 8 PM daily.

methods_participants.label: Glucose Assay Precision - Between Assays
methods_participants.value: 1.0 ng/ml maximum variation between duplicates in 5 to 15 range

methods_participants.label: Glucagon Assay Method - Separation
methods_participants.value: Trout, Estes and Friedberg method with palmitic acid standards and 4 point standard curve

evidence.quote: FFA were measured in duplicate by the method of Trout, Estes and Friedberg using palmitic acid standards and a 4 point standard curve

methods_participants.label: Insulin Assay Precision - Basal Range
methods_participants.value: Triplicate analysis with appropriate control tubes

evidence.quote: To obtain maximum accuracy within the range of normal basal values of insulin, samples were run in quadruplicate

narrative_results.statement: Plasma glucose and insulin concentrations decreased in both men and women during prolonged fasting, reaching a plateau on the second or third day of the fast

evidence.quote: With prolonged fasting, plasma glucose and insulin concentrations decreased in both men and women, reaching a plateau on the second or third day of the fast.
